---
title: "XAI 3"
output:
  word_document: default
  html_document: default
date: "2026-05-07"
---

```{r}
library(randomForest)
library(pdp)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

```

1. Data preprocessing and Summary statistics - Bike rentals
```{r}

# Load daily bike rental dataset
bike_data <- day

# Convert date variable to Date format
bike_data$dteday <- as.Date(bike_data$dteday)

# Check missing values
missing_values <- colSums(is.na(bike_data))
missing_values

# Remove missing values if any
bike_data <- na.omit(bike_data)

# Create a numerical variable representing days since 2011
bike_data <- bike_data %>%
  mutate(days_since_2011 = as.numeric(dteday - min(dteday)))

# Select variables needed for the exercise
bike_model_data <- bike_data %>%
  select(cnt, days_since_2011, temp, hum, windspeed)

# Check final dataset
summary(bike_model_data)
```

```{r}

summary_table_bike <- bike_model_data %>%
  summarise(
    n_observations = n(),
    missing_values = sum(is.na(.)),
    min_days = min(days_since_2011),
    max_days = max(days_since_2011),
    min_temp = min(temp),
    max_temp = max(temp),
    min_hum = min(hum),
    max_hum = max(hum),
    min_windspeed = min(windspeed),
    max_windspeed = max(windspeed),
    min_cnt = min(cnt),
    max_cnt = max(cnt)
  )

summary_table_bike
```
3. Random Forest model - Bike rentals
```{r}


library(randomForest)

set.seed(123)

bike_model <- randomForest(
  cnt ~ days_since_2011 + temp + hum + windspeed,
  data = bike_model_data,
  ntree = 300,
  importance = TRUE
)

# Model summary
print(bike_model)

```
```{r}
importance_bike <- importance(bike_model)

importance_bike_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(importance_bike),
  IncMSE = importance_bike[, "%IncMSE"],
  IncNodePurity = importance_bike[, "IncNodePurity"]
)

importance_bike_table <- importance_bike_table %>%
  arrange(desc(IncMSE))

importance_bike_table
```

4. One-dimensional PDPs - Bike rentals

```{r}
# -------------------------------
# 4. One-dimensional PDPs
# -------------------------------

# PDP for days since 2011
pdp_days <- partial(
  bike_model,
  pred.var = "days_since_2011",
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_days <- autoplot(pdp_days) +
  ggtitle("PDP - Days since 2011") +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for temperature
pdp_temp <- partial(
  bike_model,
  pred.var = "temp",
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_temp <- autoplot(pdp_temp) +
  ggtitle("PDP - Temperature") +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for humidity
pdp_hum <- partial(
  bike_model,
  pred.var = "hum",
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_hum <- autoplot(pdp_hum) +
  ggtitle("PDP - Humidity") +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for wind speed
pdp_wind <- partial(
  bike_model,
  pred.var = "windspeed",
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_wind <- autoplot(pdp_wind) +
  ggtitle("PDP - Wind speed") +
  theme_minimal()


# Display plots
print(plot_days)
print(plot_temp)
print(plot_hum)
print(plot_wind)
```


5. Two-dimensional PDP - Bike rentals
```{r}
# PDP 2D con temperatura y humedad
pdp_2d <- partial(bike_model,
                  pred.var = c("temp", "hum"),
                  grid.resolution = 20,
                  progress = "text")
# Visualizar como mapa de calor
pdp_plot <- ggplot(pdp_2d, aes(x = temp, y = hum, fill = yhat)) +
  geom_tile(width = 0.02, height = 0.02) +  # ancho/alto para que no haya huecos
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "plasma") +
  labs(title = "PDP 2D - Influencia conjunta de temperatura y humedad",
       x = "Temperatura normalizada",
       y = "Humedad",
       fill = "Alquileres predichos") +
  theme_minimal()

print(pdp_plot)
```
 6. Density distributions - Bike rentals

```{r}
# Tomar una muestra aleatoria para que sea más rápido
set.seed(123)  # para reproducibilidad
bike_sample <- sample_n(bike_data, 150)
# Densidad de temperatura
ggplot(bike_sample, aes(x = temp)) +
  geom_density(fill = "skyblue") +
  labs(title = "Distribución de temperatura")

# Densidad de humedad
ggplot(bike_sample, aes(x = hum)) +
  geom_density(fill = "lightgreen") +
  labs(title = "Distribución de humedad")

```
7. Data preprocessing - House prices

```{r}
house_data <- kc_house_data
# Convert date variable to Date format
house_data$date <- as.Date(house_data$date)

# Check missing values
colSums(is.na(house_data))

# Remove possible missing values
house_data <- na.omit(house_data)

# Select variables required for the exercise
house_model_data <- house_data %>%
  select(price, bedrooms, bathrooms, sqft_living, sqft_lot, floors, yr_built)

# Remove unrealistic values if present
house_model_data <- house_model_data %>%
  filter(
    price > 0,
    bedrooms > 0,
    bathrooms > 0,
    sqft_living > 0,
    sqft_lot > 0,
    floors > 0,
    yr_built > 0
  )

# Take a random sample to reduce computational cost
set.seed(123)
house_sample <- sample_n(house_model_data, 300)

# Summary of final dataset
summary(house_sample)

```
8. Random Forest model - House prices


```{r}

set.seed(123)

house_model <- randomForest(
  price ~ bedrooms + bathrooms + sqft_living + sqft_lot + floors + yr_built,
  data = house_sample,
  ntree = 300,
  importance = TRUE
)

print(house_model)


```
9. Variable importance - House prices
```{r}


# Calculate variable importance
importance_house <- importance(house_model)

# Convert importance matrix into dataframe
importance_house_table <- data.frame(
  Variable = rownames(importance_house),
  importance_house,
  row.names = NULL
)

# Display importance table
print(importance_house_table)
```

10. One-dimensional PDPs - House prices
```{r}


# PDP for bedrooms
pdp_bed <- partial(
  house_model,
  pred.var = "bedrooms",
  grid.resolution = 10
)

plot_bed <- autoplot(pdp_bed) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Bedrooms",
    x = "Number of bedrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for bathrooms
pdp_bath <- partial(
  house_model,
  pred.var = "bathrooms",
  grid.resolution = 10
)

plot_bath <- autoplot(pdp_bath) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Bathrooms",
    x = "Number of bathrooms",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for sqft_living
pdp_sqft <- partial(
  house_model,
  pred.var = "sqft_living",
  grid.resolution = 20
)

plot_sqft <- autoplot(pdp_sqft) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Sqft Living",
    x = "Living area (sqft)",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# PDP for floors
pdp_floors <- partial(
  house_model,
  pred.var = "floors",
  grid.resolution = 10
)

plot_floors <- autoplot(pdp_floors) +
  labs(
    title = "PDP - Floors",
    x = "Number of floors",
    y = "Predicted house price"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# Display plots
print(plot_bed)
print(plot_bath)
print(plot_sqft)
print(plot_floors)
```
